perturbation.original_text: "Territory" shall mean the geographical area under the jurisdiction of the Government of the Republic of India. 1.2 Interpretation: In this Agreement, unless the contrary intention appears, a reference to: i) an "amendment" includes a supplement, novation, replacement or re-enactment and "amended" is to be construed accordingly; ii) "assets" includes properties (both present and future and whether tangible or intangible and including Intellectual Property and Intellectual Property Rights), revenues, investments, cash flows, rights, benefits, interests and titles of every description; iii) an "authorisation" includes an authorisation, consent, clearance, approval, resolution, license, permit, exemption, filing, registration or notarisation;
perturbation.changed_text: "Territory" shall mean the geographical area under the jurisdiction of the Government of the Republic of India and any other country as mutually agreed upon by both parties. 1.2 Interpretation: In this Agreement, unless the contrary intention appears, a reference to: i) an "amendment" includes a supplement, novation, replacement or re-enactment and "amended" is to be construed accordingly; ii) "assets" includes properties (both present and future and whether tangible or intangible and including Intellectual Property and Intellectual Property Rights), revenues, investments, cash flows, rights, benefits, interests and titles of every description; iii) an "authorisation" includes an authorisation, consent, clearance, approval, resolution, license, permit, exemption, filing, registration or notarisation;
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Territory' has been modified to include not only India but also any other country mutually agreed upon by both parties. This creates an in-text contradiction with Article 14.1, where IMPCO agrees not to enter into any other Joint Venture Agreement or Marketing/Distribution Agreement in 'the Territory', which originally referred to only India. By expanding the definition of 'Territory', the non-compete clause now ambiguously covers other countries, potentially restricting IMPCO's business activities in those regions without clear agreement, leading to a contractual conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IMPCO expressly agrees that during the existence of this Agreement, IMPCO shall not enter into any other Joint Venture Agreement or Marketing/Distribution Agreement, with any company or person(s) in the Territory with respect to the Products.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Territory' now includes potentially any country, which directly conflicts with Article 14.1. Article 14.1 explicitly states that IMPCO shall not enter into any other Joint Venture Agreement or Marketing/Distribution Agreement in 'the Territory' with respect to the Products. The original definition limited 'the Territory' to India, offering a clear geographic scope. The modified definition expands this scope, leading to ambiguity and potentially restricting IMPCO's activities in other countries without specific agreement, thus directly contradicting the previously defined restriction within the original scope of India only.

perturbation.original_text: 16A.1 The Parties agree that in the event of termination of this Agreement due to any of the reasons mentioned in Article 16 above (and consequent termination of the TAA) the JVC and/or MINDA/MIL will not be entitled to use the Technical Know-How for any reason whatsoever. Provided, however, that in the event the termination of this Agreement is followed by a sale of all of the shares held by IMPCO in the JVC to MINDA/MIL, the JVC will have a right to continue to use the Technical Know How already received and absorbed on the condition that royalty payments under the TAA have been paid by the JVC to IMPCO or will be paid, as the case may be, for a minimum period of five (5) years, as provided in the TAA.
perturbation.changed_text: 16A.1 The Parties agree that in the event of termination of this Agreement due to any of the reasons mentioned in Article 16 above (and consequent termination of the TAA) the JVC and/or MINDA/MIL will be entitled to use the Technical Know-How for any reason whatsoever, regardless of the conditions of termination. Provided, however, that in the event the termination of this Agreement is followed by a sale of all of the shares held by IMPCO in the JVC to MINDA/MIL, the JVC will have a right to continue to use the Technical Know How already received and absorbed on the condition that royalty payments under the TAA have been paid by the JVC to IMPCO or will be paid, as the case may be, for a minimum period of five (5) years, as provided in the TAA.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the phrase 'will not be entitled to use the Technical Know-How' to 'will be entitled to use the Technical Know-How', Article 16A.1 creates an in-text contradiction. It initially restricts the JVC and/or MINDA/MIL from using the Technical Know-How after termination but is modified to allow them to use it regardless of termination conditions. This new term undermines the conditions about termination and sale of shares which were the requirements and considerations, and that can cause confusion in the enforcement of know-how usage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree that in the event of termination of this Agreement due to any of the reasons mentioned in Article 16 above (and consequent termination of the TAA) the JVC and/or MINDA/MIL will be entitled to use the Technical Know-How for any reason whatsoever, regardless of the conditions of termination. Provided, however, that in the event the termination of this Agreement is followed by a sale of all of the shares held by IMPCO in the JVC to MINDA/MIL, the JVC will have a right to continue to use the Technical Know How already received and absorbed on the condition that royalty payments under the TAA have been paid by the JVC to IMPCO or will be paid, as the case may be, for a minimum period of five (5) years, as provided in the TAA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The modified Article 16A.1 initially states that upon termination, JVC and/or MINDA/MIL are entitled to use the Technical Know-How 'for any reason whatsoever, regardless of the conditions of termination'. However, the subsequent sentence introduces a condition: 'Provided, however, that in the event the termination of this Agreement is followed by a sale of all of the shares held by IMPCO in the JVC to MINDA/MIL, the JVC will have a right to continue to use the Technical Know How already received and absorbed on the condition that royalty payments under the TAA have been paid by the JVC to IMPCO.' This creates a conflict because the first part says they are entitled regardless of conditions, while the second part introduces a specific condition related to the sale of shares and royalty payments. This contradicts the initial claim of unconditional entitlement. Therefore, it creates an inconsistency as described in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 All management decisions of the JVC shall require the simple majority vote of the Board either at a meeting of the Board or by resolution passed by circulation subject to the provisions of the Article 5.6 Meetings of the Board shall take place in Delhi or such other place as may be decided by the Board. For calling a Board meeting, a notice of at least fourteen [14 ] days in advance in writing shall be given to each of the Directors, including those outside India. However, a meeting of Directors may be called by giving a shorter notice provided it is mutually agreed by the Directors. Subject to the provisions of the Act, a resolution circulated and signed by all Directors shall be effective, as a resolution duly passed at a meeting of Directors. Sitting fees, travel and stay expenses (incurred by the Directors) for attending the Board meeting, if claimed by the Director(s) shall be met by the JVC as per Articles of Association of the JVC.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 All management decisions of the JVC shall require the simple majority vote of the Board either at a meeting of the Board or by resolution passed by circulation subject to the provisions of the Article 5.6. However, certain critical decisions, as defined in Schedule II, shall require unanimous consent of all directors. Meetings of the Board shall take place in Delhi or such other place as may be decided by the Board. For calling a Board meeting, a notice of at least fourteen [14 ] days in advance in writing shall be given to each of the Directors, including those outside India. However, a meeting of Directors may be called by giving a shorter notice provided it is mutually agreed by the Directors. Subject to the provisions of the Act, a resolution circulated and signed by all Directors shall be effective, as a resolution duly passed at a meeting of Directors. Sitting fees, travel and stay expenses (incurred by the Directors) for attending the Board meeting, if claimed by the Director(s) shall be met by the JVC as per Articles of Association of the JVC.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, certain critical decisions, as defined in Schedule II, shall require unanimous consent of all directors' creates an in-text contradiction with the initial statement that 'All management decisions of the JVC shall require the simple majority vote of the Board'. This introduces ambiguity regarding which decisions require a simple majority versus unanimous consent, potentially leading to disputes over the validity of board decisions and hindering effective management.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All management decisions of the JVC shall require the simple majority vote of the Board either at a meeting of the Board or by resolution passed by circulation subject to the provisions of the Article 5.6. However, certain critical decisions, as defined in Schedule II, shall require unanimous consent of all directors. Meetings of the Board shall take place in Delhi or such other place as may be decided by the Board. For calling a Board meeting, a notice of at least fourteen [14 ] days in advance in writing shall be given to each of the Directors, including those outside India. However, a meeting of Directors may be called by giving a shorter notice provided it is mutually agreed by the Directors. Subject to the provisions of the Act, a resolution circulated and signed by all Directors shall be effective, as a resolution duly passed at a meeting of Directors. Sitting fees, travel and stay expenses (incurred by the Directors) for attending the Board meeting, if claimed by the Director(s) shall be met by the JVC as per Articles of Association of the JVC.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'All management decisions of the JVC shall require the simple majority vote of the Board'. The changed text adds 'However, certain critical decisions, as defined in Schedule II, shall require unanimous consent of all directors.' This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency because it's unclear which management decisions require simple majority and which require unanimous consent. Both sentences cannot be true simultaneously without further clarification which is lacking. This is an in-text contradiction under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
